differences.justification: If ToolProviderResult were extended and the subclass already declared a constructor with this same signature at lesser visibility, this change would be a breaking change. ToolProviderResult is not expected to be extended, so this is acceptable.
@Internal removed intentionally: AiServiceTool is now part of the public API and can be constructed and passed to AiServices directly by users (e.g. via AiServices.tools(List<AiServiceTool>)).

GuardrailService is implemented only internally (AbstractGuardrailService/DefaultGuardrailService) and constructed via the builder SPI; its primary execute methods are already abstract. Adding the non-blocking executeInputGuardrailsAsync counterpart mirrors the existing abstract executeInputGuardrails and is not a break for API consumers, who implement Guardrail/InputGuardrail/OutputGuardrail rather than GuardrailService.

GuardrailService is implemented only internally (AbstractGuardrailService/DefaultGuardrailService) and constructed via the builder SPI; its primary execute methods are already abstract. Adding the non-blocking executeOutputGuardrailsAsync counterpart mirrors the existing abstract executeOutputGuardrails and is not a break for API consumers, who implement Guardrail/InputGuardrail/OutputGuardrail rather than GuardrailService.
